reasoning: B leads on calibration and bias awareness: it states explicit, sensibly-tiered probabilities (60-70% conditional vs 25-35% unconditional, 40-60% match), steelmans then base-rate-checks the 'incumbent will match' claim, and uniquely names that both advisors committed the same single-branch error, capped with a four-cell payoff, premortem, and pre-committed kill criteria. A matches B on the structural reasoning (extrapolation, confound, Lucas-critique 'frozen world') and backward induction with a sharp wedge-vs-price verdict, but expresses uncertainty only qualitatively. C contributes the decision-critical arithmetic the others omit (a 15% cut needs ~17.6% volume to hold revenue, ~43% to protect contribution at 50% margin), a genuine denominator insight, but lacks a game tree and explicit probabilities. D is sound and names the Lucas critique with a numeric fork, but reads as a near-subset of B without B's calibration or the dual-advisor-error framing.
